content: Laravel scheduler in Docker containers: (1) Current TenantSmith has NO scheduler service in either compose file. routes/console.php defines domains:sync-cloudflare but it is not scheduled. (2) Best approach for Docker: add a dedicated 'scheduler' service running 'php artisan schedule:work' (Laravel 11+ recommended approach). (3) schedule:work runs the scheduler every minute in the foreground - ideal for Docker because it is a long-running foreground process (no cron daemon needed). (4) Alternative cron approach requires cron installed in container and a crontab entry - more complex, harder to log, no stdout. (5) Supervisor approach is overkill for single scheduler. (6) The scheduler service should share the same image as app/queue, just different command. (7) Consider scheduling domains:sync-cloudflare --all for periodic Cloudflare status sweep.
